2606.06212.title: Evaluating Agentic Configuration Repair for Computer Networks
2606.06212.paper_summary: Misconfigurations in computer networks remain a major source of critical Internet outages. Research is turning to Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the complex, error-prone task of network configuration. However, even state-of-the-art models fail to resolve misconfigurations in large-scale, complex scenarios and often introduce new errors. In this work, we benchmark open- and closed-source LLMs augmented with formal network verification and context retrieval tools. We demonstrate that agentic architectures outperform base LLMs in repair efficacy (by 12% on average) and safety (by 17% on average), enabled by the ability to dynamically manage context and iteratively validate configuration repairs.
2606.06212.paper_summary_zh: 計算機網絡中的配置錯誤仍然是導致關鍵互聯網中斷的主要來源。研究正在轉向大型語言模型（LLMs）以自動化這一複雜且容易出錯的網絡配置任務。然而，即使是最先進的模型在大規模、複雜的場景中也無法解決配置錯誤，並且經常引入新的錯誤。在這項工作中，我們基準測試了增強了正式網絡驗證和上下文檢索工具的開源和閉源LLMs。我們證明，代理架構在修復效果（平均提高12%）和安全性（平均提高17%）方面超越了基礎LLMs，這得益於能夠動態管理上下文並迭代驗證配置修復的能力。
2606.06212.author: Rufat Asadli et.al.
2606.06212.authors: Rufat Asadli, Benjamin Hoffman, Ioannis Protogeros, Laurent Vanbever
